div.head: The Minister in Egypt (Kirk) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
1317. Personal for the Secretary and Under Secretary. The continuation of the hitherto indecisive fighting on the El Alamein front has furnished occasion for professing of optimism as to the outcome of the struggle and an inclination is apparent to exalt Allied achievement and discount the growing strength of the enemy. This attitude is unjustified and fundamentally unhealthy. Reports are current of
the mounting reinforcements to Rommel’s army and in spite of the efforts which have apparently been made, there is no outstanding improvement in the general position of the British. A few days ago operations were initiated by AuchinleckGen. Sir Claude J. E. Auchinleck, Commanding General, British Forces in the Middle East. which it is difficult to believe were not intended to attain a decisive objective but those operations halted at an inconclusive state. It is now said that they may be resumed at any moment. I fail to see, however, any new and definite factor which in itself offers encouragement for the future.
